{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Closed Sicilian| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| | |pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | |pd| | | | |= | | |pd| | | | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |nl| | | | | |= |pl|pl|pl|pl| |pl|pl|pl|= |rl| |bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} = Closed Sicilian = This move is a perfectly reasonable move, often favored by Najdorf players to avoid some move-order tricks like 2...Nc6 3.Nf3 when White firmly avoids the Najdorf, but has the downside that ...d5 would cost a tempo since Black has already played ...d6. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}.
